yes.n: asked the Mock Turtle a little anxiously.
yes.s: 'Yes,' said Alice, 'we learned French and music.'

yes.n: 'Well, then,' the Gryphon went on, 'if you don't know what to uglify is, you ARE a simpleton.'
'I don't know where Dinn may be,' said the Mock Turtle, 'but if you've seen them so often, of course you know what they're like.'
yes.s: 'Yes,' said Alice, 'I've often seen them at dinn--' she checked herself hastily.

yes.n: 'Shall we try another figure of the Lobster Quadrille?'
yes.s: 'Yes, I think you'd better leave off,' said the Gryphon: and Alice was only too glad to do so.

yes.n: And he got up very sulkily and crossed over to the other side of the court.
yes.s: 'Yes, but I grow at a reasonable pace,' said the Dormouse: 'not in that ridiculous fashion.'

yesterday.n: I wonder if I've been changed in the night?
yesterday.s: And yesterday things went on just as usual.
I heard the Queen say only yesterday you deserved to be beheaded!'

yesterday.n: 'Explain all that,' said the Mock Turtle.
yesterday.s: 'I could tell you my adventures--beginning from this morning,' said Alice a little timidly: 'but it's no use going back to yesterday, because I was a different person then.'

young.n: 'In my youth,' Father William replied to his son, 'I feared it might injure the brain; But, now that I'm perfectly sure I have none, Why, I do it again and again.'
young.s: 'You are old, Father William,' the young man said, 'And your hair has become very white; And yet you incessantly stand on your head-- Do you think, at your age, it is right?'

young.n: 'I'm afraid I don't know one,' said Alice, rather alarmed at the proposal.
young.s: I vote the young lady tells us a story.'

young.n: I must go back and see after some executions I have ordered'; and she walked off, leaving Alice alone with the Gryphon.
young.s: said the Queen, 'and take this young lady to see the Mock Turtle, and to hear his history.

young.n: 'I'll tell it her,' said the Mock Turtle in a deep, hollow tone: 'sit down, both of you, and don't speak a word till I've finished.'
young.s: 'This here young lady,' said the Gryphon, 'she wants for to know your history, she do.'

youth.n: 'You are old,' said the youth, 'as I mentioned before, And have grown most uncommonly fat; Yet you turned a back-somersault in at the door-- Pray, what is the reason of that?'
youth.s: 'In my youth,' Father William replied to his son, 'I feared it might injure the brain; But, now that I'm perfectly sure I have none, Why, I do it again and again.'

youth.n: 'In my youth,' said the sage, as he shook his grey locks, 'I kept all my limbs very supple By the use of this ointment--one shilling the box-- Allow me to sell you a couple?'
youth.s: 'You are old,' said the youth, 'as I mentioned before, And have grown most uncommonly fat; Yet you turned a back-somersault in at the door-- Pray, what is the reason of that?'

youth.n: 'You are old,' said the youth, 'and your jaws are too weak For anything tougher than suet; Yet you finished the goose, with the bones and the beak-- Pray how did you manage to do it?'
youth.s: 'In my youth,' said the sage, as he shook his grey locks, 'I kept all my limbs very supple By the use of this ointment--one shilling the box-- Allow me to sell you a couple?'

youth.n: 'In my youth,' said his father, 'I took to the law, And argued each case with my wife; And the muscular strength, which it gave to my jaw, Has lasted the rest of my life.'
youth.s: 'You are old,' said the youth, 'and your jaws are too weak For anything tougher than suet; Yet you finished the goose, with the bones and the beak-- Pray how did you manage to do it?'

youth.n: 'You are old,' said the youth, 'one would hardly suppose That your eye was as steady as ever; Yet you balanced an eel on the end of your nose-- What made you so awfully clever?'
youth.s: 'In my youth,' said his father, 'I took to the law, And argued each case with my wife; And the muscular strength, which it gave to my jaw, Has lasted the rest of my life.'

youth.n: 'I have answered three questions, and that is enough,' Said his father; 'don't give yourself airs!
youth.s: 'You are old,' said the youth, 'one would hardly suppose That your eye was as steady as ever; Yet you balanced an eel on the end of your nose-- What made you so awfully clever?'
